<a name="Marshal"></a>
## func [Marshal](<https://github.com/mailru/easyjson/blob/master/helpers.go#L52>)

```go
func Marshal(v Marshaler) ([]byte, error)
```

Marshal returns data as a single byte slice. Method is suboptimal as the data is likely to be copied from a chain of smaller chunks.

<a name="MarshalToHTTPResponseWriter"></a>
## func [MarshalToHTTPResponseWriter](<https://github.com/mailru/easyjson/blob/master/helpers.go#L77>)

```go
func MarshalToHTTPResponseWriter(v Marshaler, w http.ResponseWriter) (started bool, written int, err error)
```

MarshalToHTTPResponseWriter sets Content\-Length and Content\-Type headers for the http.ResponseWriter, and send the data to the writer. started will be equal to false if an error occurred before any http.ResponseWriter methods were actually invoked \(in this case a 500 reply is possible\).

<a name="MarshalToWriter"></a>

```go
func MarshalToWriter(v Marshaler, w io.Writer) (written int, err error)
```

MarshalToWriter marshals the data to an io.Writer.

<a name="RawMessage"></a>
## type [RawMessage](<https://github.com/mailru/easyjson/blob/master/raw.go#L10>)

RawMessage is a raw piece of JSON \(number, string, bool, object, array or null\) that is extracted without parsing and output as is during marshaling.

```go
type RawMessage []byte
```

# benchmark

```go
import "github.com/mailru/easyjson/benchmark"
```

Package benchmark provides a simple benchmark for easyjson against default serialization and ffjson. The data example is taken from https://dev.twitter.com/rest/reference/get/search/tweets

# bootstrap

```go
import "github.com/mailru/easyjson/bootstrap"
```

Package bootstrap implements the bootstrapping logic: generation of a .go file to launch the actual generator and launching the generator itself.

The package may be preferred to a command\-line utility if generating the serializers from golang code is required.

## Index

- [type Generator](<#Generator>)
  - [func \(g \*Generator\) Run\(\) error](<#Generator.Run>)


<a name="Generator"></a>
## type [Generator](<https://github.com/mailru/easyjson/blob/master/bootstrap/bootstrap.go#L25-L44>)



```go
type Generator struct {
    PkgPath, PkgName string
    Types            []string

    NoStdMarshalers          bool
    SnakeCase                bool
    LowerCamelCase           bool
    OmitEmpty                bool
    DisallowUnknownFields    bool
    SkipMemberNameUnescaping bool

    OutName       string
    BuildTags     string
    GenBuildFlags string

    StubsOnly   bool
    LeaveTemps  bool
    NoFormat    bool
    SimpleBytes bool
}
```

### func \(\*Generator\) [Run](<https://github.com/mailru/easyjson/blob/master/bootstrap/bootstrap.go#L161>)

```go
func (g *Generator) Run() error
```

# buffer

```go
import "github.com/mailru/easyjson/buffer"
```

Package buffer implements a buffer for serialization, consisting of a chain of \[\]byte\-s to reduce copying and to allow reuse of individual chunks.

<a name="Init"></a>
## func [Init](<https://github.com/mailru/easyjson/blob/master/buffer/pool.go#L38>)

```go
func Init(cfg PoolConfig)
```

Init sets up a non\-default pooling and allocation strategy. Should be run before serialization is done.

<a name="Buffer"></a>
## type [Buffer](<https://github.com/mailru/easyjson/blob/master/buffer/pool.go#L68-L75>)

Buffer is a buffer optimized for serialization without extra copying.

```go
type Buffer struct {

    // Buf is the current chunk that can be used for serialization.
    Buf []byte
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="Buffer.AppendByte"></a>
### func \(\*Buffer\) [AppendByte](<https://github.com/mailru/easyjson/blob/master/buffer/pool.go#L109>)

```go
func (b *Buffer) AppendByte(data byte)
```

AppendByte appends a single byte to buffer.

### func \(\*Buffer\) [AppendBytes](<https://github.com/mailru/easyjson/blob/master/buffer/pool.go#L115>)

```go
func (b *Buffer) AppendBytes(data []byte)
```

AppendBytes appends a byte slice to buffer.

<a name="Buffer.AppendString"></a>
### func \(\*Buffer\) [AppendString](<https://github.com/mailru/easyjson/blob/master/buffer/pool.go#L138>)

```go
func (b *Buffer) AppendString(data string)
```

AppendString appends a string to buffer.

<a name="Buffer.BuildBytes"></a>
### func \(\*Buffer\) [BuildBytes](<https://github.com/mailru/easyjson/blob/master/buffer/pool.go#L192>)

```go
func (b *Buffer) BuildBytes(reuse ...[]byte) []byte
```

BuildBytes creates a single byte slice with all the contents of the buffer. Data is copied if it does not fit in a single chunk. You can optionally provide one byte slice as argument that it will try to reuse.

<a name="Buffer.DumpTo"></a>
### func \(\*Buffer\) [DumpTo](<https://github.com/mailru/easyjson/blob/master/buffer/pool.go#L170>)

```go
func (b *Buffer) DumpTo(w io.Writer) (written int, err error)
```

DumpTo outputs the contents of a buffer to a writer and resets the buffer.

<a name="Buffer.EnsureSpace"></a>
### func \(\*Buffer\) [EnsureSpace](<https://github.com/mailru/easyjson/blob/master/buffer/pool.go#L79>)

```go
func (b *Buffer) EnsureSpace(s int)
```

EnsureSpace makes sure that the current chunk contains at least s free bytes, possibly creating a new chunk.

<a name="Buffer.ReadCloser"></a>
### func \(\*Buffer\) [ReadCloser](<https://github.com/mailru/easyjson/blob/master/buffer/pool.go#L270>)

```go

```

```go
func (b *Buffer) Size() int
```

<a name="PoolConfig"></a>
## type [PoolConfig](<https://github.com/mailru/easyjson/blob/master/buffer/pool.go#L12-L16>)

PoolConfig contains configuration for the allocation and reuse strategy.

```go
type PoolConfig struct {
    StartSize  int // Minimum chunk size that is allocated.
    PooledSize int // Minimum chunk size that is reused, reusing chunks too small will result in overhead.
    MaxSize    int // Maximum chunk size that will be allocated.
}
```

# jlexer

```go
import "github.com/mailru/easyjson/jlexer"
```

Package jlexer contains a JSON lexer implementation.

It is expected that it is mostly used with generated parser code, so the interface is tuned for a parser that knows what kind of data is expected.

<a name="Lexer"></a>
## type [Lexer](<https://github.com/mailru/easyjson/blob/master/jlexer/lexer.go#L45-L58>)

Lexer is a JSON lexer: it iterates over JSON tokens in a byte slice.

```go
type Lexer struct {
    Data []byte // Input data given to the lexer.

    UseMultipleErrors bool // If we want to use multiple errors.
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

```go
func (r *Lexer) UnsafeString() string
```

UnsafeString returns the string value if the token is a string literal.

Warning: returned string may point to the input buffer, so the string should not outlive the input buffer. Intended pattern of usage is as an argument to a switch statement.

<a name="LexerError"></a>
## type [LexerError](<https://github.com/mailru/easyjson/blob/master/jlexer/error.go#L7-L11>)

LexerError implements the error interface and represents all possible errors that can be generated during parsing the JSON data.

```go
type LexerError struct {
    Reason string
    Offset int
    Data   string
}
```

<a name="LexerError.Error"></a>
### func \(\*LexerError\) [Error](<https://github.com/mailru/easyjson/blob/master/jlexer/error.go#L13>)

```go
func (l *LexerError) Error() string
```



<a name="TokenKind"></a>
## type [TokenKind](<https://github.com/mailru/easyjson/blob/master/jlexer/lexer.go#L23>)

TokenKind determines type of a token.

```go
type TokenKind byte
```

<a name="TokenUndef"></a>

```go
const (
    TokenUndef  TokenKind = iota // No token.
    TokenDelim                   // Delimiter: one of '{', '}', '[' or ']'.
    TokenString                  // A string literal, e.g. "abc\u1234"
    TokenNumber                  // Number literal, e.g. 1.5e5
    TokenBool                    // Boolean literal: true or false.
    TokenNull                    // null keyword.
)
```

<a name="Flags"></a>
## type [Flags](<https://github.com/mailru/easyjson/blob/master/jwriter/writer.go#L16>)

Flags describe various encoding options. The behavior may be actually implemented in the encoder, but Flags field in Writer is used to set and pass them around.

```go
type Flags int
```

<a name="NilMapAsEmpty"></a>

```go
const (
    NilMapAsEmpty   Flags = 1 << iota // Encode nil map as '{}' rather than 'null'.
    NilSliceAsEmpty                   // Encode nil slice as '[]' rather than 'null'.
)
```

<a name="Writer"></a>
## type [Writer](<https://github.com/mailru/easyjson/blob/master/jwriter/writer.go#L24-L30>)

Writer is a JSON writer.

```go
type Writer struct {
    Flags Flags

    Error        error
    Buffer       buffer.Buffer
    NoEscapeHTML bool
}
```

<a name="Writer.Base64Bytes"></a>
### func \(\*Writer\) [Base64Bytes](<https://github.com/mailru/easyjson/blob/master/jwriter/writer.go#L101>)

```go
func (w *Writer) Base64Bytes(data []byte)
```

Base64Bytes appends data to the buffer after base64 encoding it

<a name="Writer.Bool"></a>
### func \(\*Writer\) [Bool](<https://github.com/mailru/easyjson/blob/master/jwriter/writer.go#L278>)

```go
func (w *Writer) Bool(v bool)
```



<a name="Writer.BuildBytes"></a>
### func \(\*Writer\) [BuildBytes](<https://github.com/mailru/easyjson/blob/master/jwriter/writer.go#L44>)

```go
func (w *Writer) BuildBytes(reuse ...[]byte) ([]byte, error)
```

BuildBytes returns writer data as a single byte slice. You can optionally provide one byte slice as argument that it will try to reuse.

<a name="Writer.DumpTo"></a>
### func \(\*Writer\) [DumpTo](<https://github.com/mailru/easyjson/blob/master/jwriter/writer.go#L38>)

```go
func (w *Writer) DumpTo(out io.Writer) (written int, err error)
```

DumpTo outputs the data to given io.Writer, resetting the buffer.

### func \(\*Writer\) [Raw](<https://github.com/mailru/easyjson/blob/master/jwriter/writer.go#L74>)

```go
func (w *Writer) Raw(data []byte, err error)
```

Raw appends raw binary data to the buffer or sets the error if it is given. Useful for calling with results of MarshalJSON\-like functions.

### func \(\*Writer\) [RawByte](<https://github.com/mailru/easyjson/blob/master/jwriter/writer.go#L63>)

```go
func (w *Writer) RawByte(c byte)
```

### func \(\*Writer\) [RawString](<https://github.com/mailru/easyjson/blob/master/jwriter/writer.go#L68>)

```go

```

RawByte appends raw binary data to the buffer.

<a name="Writer.RawText"></a>
### func \(\*Writer\) [RawText](<https://github.com/mailru/easyjson/blob/master/jwriter/writer.go#L89>)

```go
func (w *Writer) RawText(data []byte, err error)
```

RawText encloses raw binary data in quotes and appends in to the buffer. Useful for calling with results of MarshalText\-like functions.


ReadCloser returns an io.ReadCloser that can be used to read the data. ReadCloser also resets the buffer.

<a name="Bool"></a>
## type [Bool](<https://github.com/mailru/easyjson/blob/master/opt/gotemplate_Bool.go#L15-L18>)

A 'gotemplate'\-based type for providing optional semantics without using pointers.

```go
type Bool struct {
    V       bool
    Defined bool
}
```

<a name="OBool"></a>
### func [OBool](<https://github.com/mailru/easyjson/blob/master/opt/gotemplate_Bool.go#L21>)

```go
func OBool(v bool) Bool
```

Creates an optional type with a given value.

<a name="Bool.Get"></a>
### func \(Bool\) [Get](<https://github.com/mailru/easyjson/blob/master/opt/gotemplate_Bool.go#L26>)

```go
func (v Bool) Get(deflt bool) bool
```

Get returns the value or given default in the case the value is undefined.

<a name="Bool.IsDefined"></a>
### func \(Bool\) [IsDefined](<https://github.com/mailru/easyjson/blob/master/opt/gotemplate_Bool.go#L69>)

```go
func (v Bool) IsDefined() bool
```

IsDefined returns whether the value is defined, a function is required so that it can be used in an interface.

<a name="Bool.MarshalEasyJSON"></a>
### func \(Bool\) [MarshalEasyJSON](<https://github.com/mailru/easyjson/blob/master/opt/gotemplate_Bool.go#L34>)

```go
func (v Bool) MarshalEasyJSON(w *jwriter.Writer)
```

MarshalEasyJSON does JSON marshaling using easyjson interface.

<a name="Bool.MarshalJSON"></a>
### func \(Bool\) [MarshalJSON](<https://github.com/mailru/easyjson/blob/master/opt/gotemplate_Bool.go#L54>)

```go
func (v Bool) MarshalJSON() ([]byte, error)
```
